config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary, because that is how abuse by governments or majorities can happen. From my perspective, the right balance is that society can place limits only when there is a clear, fair, and necessary reason, not just because it is convenient. In Indonesia, I have seen how easily “public interest” can be used to pressure ordinary people, so I am cautious about giving society automatic priority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya sangat curiga pada klaim “demi kepentingan umum” karena sejarah kami penuh contoh ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah memakai alasan itu untuk membatasi kebebasan sipil, dan itu sering merugikan orang yang paling rentan. Bagi saya, hak individu harus jadi batas utama, sementara kepentingan masyarakat baru boleh mengalahkannya dalam keadaan yang benar-benar perlu, sempit, dan dapat dibenarkan secara adil. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alasan untuk menyalahgunakan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective in Indonesia, I am very wary of any argument that sounds like “for the public good” because it can easily become a cover for pressure, corruption, or abuse by people in power. Society does need limits and shared responsibilities, but those limits must be narrow, fair, and clearly necessary, otherwise the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice. For me, the real challenge is not choosing society over the person, but making sure society serves people without trampling their rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, bukan dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan sipil, dan itu paling berbahaya saat mayoritas merasa suaranya otomatis paling benar. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi hanya boleh mengalahkan hak individu dalam keadaan yang sangat sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu. Kalau tidak, istilah “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menyalahgunakan kekuasaan dan mengorbankan orang yang tidak punya perlindungan politik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement, because in my view society should serve people, not override them by default. From an Indonesian perspective, I am especially cautious about “public interest” arguments, since they are often used to pressure ordinary people and excuse unfair treatment when power is concentrated in the wrong hands. Society does need common rules for safety and order, but those rules should only limit individual rights when the need is clear, proportionate, and truly justified. If we put society first in a blanket way, it becomes too easy to sacrifice minority voices and call it the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; bagi saya, kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Sebagai orang dari Amerika Serikat, saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan kebebasan sipil, dan itu biasanya paling merugikan minoritas atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, bukan sebaliknya, kecuali dalam keadaan yang sangat sempit, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
